subject: Product Management
title: The Value Hypothesis
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Value Hypothesis.

hook.text: Before you can find PMF, you need a value hypothesis: a clear bet about what value you're creating for whom.

buildup.text: The value hypothesis states: 'We believe [customer segment] will [use/pay for] our product because [specific value].'

discovery.text: A strong value hypothesis is specific and falsifiable. 'Everyone will love it' isn't a hypothesis — it's a wish.

twist.text: Most failed products had no clear value hypothesis. The team built features without articulating who would benefit and why.

climax.text: Testing your value hypothesis is the fastest path to PMF. Every experiment should confirm or invalidate part of it.

punchline.text: State your bet clearly. Then test it ruthlessly.

quiz.question: What is a value hypothesis?
options: A specific, testable bet about what value you're creating for whom
A general statement about market size
